metadata.description: Essential vocabulary for traveling in China — hiking in Zhangjiajie, eating in Chongqing, and navigating Shanghai

content.name: hike_climb_mountain
content.side_a: To hike; to climb a mountain
content.side_e: Wǒmen míngtiān qù pá shān ba!
content.side_g: 爬 means 'to climb/crawl' and 山 means 'mountain.' Together 爬山 is the standard expression for hiking or mountain climbing. In Zhangjiajie you'll hear this constantly. Can also say 爬山步道 (pá shān bùdào) for 'hiking trail.' Use 去爬山 to say 'go hiking.'

content.name: hot_pot
content.side_f: Chongqing's hot pot is extremely famous!
content.side_g: 火 means 'fire' and 锅 means 'pot.' Chongqing-style hot pot (重庆火锅) is famously spicy with a 麻辣 (numbing-spicy) broth. You can ask for 鸳鸯锅 (yuānyāng guō, 'mandarin duck pot') which is a split pot — half spicy, half mild. Essential vocabulary for eating in Chongqing.

content.name: spicy
content.side_e: Zhège cài tài là le! Wǒ chī bù liǎo.
content.side_f: This dish is too spicy! I can't eat it.
content.side_g: Critical word for Chongqing and Sichuan cuisine. 不辣 (bú là) means 'not spicy' — useful when ordering. 微辣 (wēi là) means 'mildly spicy,' 中辣 (zhōng là) means 'medium spicy,' and 特辣 (tè là) means 'extra spicy.' In Chongqing, 麻辣 (málà) combines numbing (Sichuan peppercorn) and spicy (chili).

content.name: delicious
content.side_g: 好 (good) + 吃 (eat) = literally 'good to eat.' One of the most useful compliments for food. For drinks, use 好喝 (hǎohē, 'good to drink'). 不好吃 means 'not tasty.' 很好吃 and 真好吃 are common intensified forms. You'll use this constantly at restaurants.

content.name: menu
content.side_f: Please give me the menu, thank you.
content.side_g: 菜 means 'dishes/food' and 单 means 'list/slip.' At restaurants, say 请给我菜单 to ask for the menu. Many restaurants now use QR codes on the table — you might hear 扫码点餐 (sǎo mǎ diǎn cān, 'scan code to order'). Some places have picture menus which is helpful for pointing.

content.name: restroom
content.side_f: Excuse me, where is the restroom?
content.side_g: Possibly the most essential travel word. 厕所在哪儿？(Cèsuǒ zài nǎr?) is the go-to phrase. Signs may also say 卫生间 (wèishēngjiān, more formal) or 洗手间 (xǐshǒujiān, 'hand-washing room'). On hiking trails, look for signs saying 公共厕所 (gōnggòng cèsuǒ, 'public restroom').

content.name: scenery
content.side_e: Zhāngjiājiè de fēngjǐng tài měi le!
content.side_f: The scenery in Zhangjiajie is so beautiful!
content.side_g: 风 means 'wind' and 景 means 'scene/view.' 风景 is the general word for natural scenery and landscapes. 风景区 (fēngjǐng qū) means 'scenic area' — you'll see this on signs at parks and tourist spots. 风景很好 means 'the scenery is great.' Also common: 景色 (jǐngsè, 'scenery/view').

content.name: travel_tourism
content.side_e: Wǒ hé wǒ tàitai qù Zhōngguó lǚyóu.
content.side_f: My wife and I are traveling to China.
content.side_g: 旅 means 'travel/journey' and 游 means 'tour/roam.' 旅游 works as both verb and noun. 旅游景点 (lǚyóu jǐngdiǎn) means 'tourist attraction.' 旅游团 (lǚyóu tuán) means 'tour group.' You can say 我来旅游的 (wǒ lái lǚyóu de, 'I'm here for travel') when locals ask why you're visiting.

content.name: take_photo
content.side_e: Kěyǐ bāng wǒmen zhào xiàng ma?
content.side_f: Can you help us take a photo?
content.side_g: 照 means 'to shine/illuminate' and 相 here means 'appearance/photo.' 照相机 (zhàoxiàngjī) means 'camera.' To ask someone to take your photo: 可以帮我们照相吗？Also commonly heard: 拍照 (pāi zhào, 'take a photo') — slightly more colloquial. At scenic spots, you'll use this a lot.

content.name: be_careful
content.side_e: Pá shān de shíhou yào xiǎoxīn!
content.side_g: 小 means 'small' and 心 means 'heart' — literally 'small heart,' meaning cautious. Essential for hiking trails. You'll see signs saying 小心滑 (xiǎoxīn huá, 'caution: slippery') or 小心台阶 (xiǎoxīn táijiē, 'watch your step'). You can warn your wife: 小心！路很滑 (Xiǎoxīn! Lù hěn huá — 'Careful! The path is slippery').

content.name: water
content.side_e: Qǐng gěi wǒ yì píng shuǐ.
content.side_f: Please give me a bottle of water.
content.side_g: Absolutely critical when hiking. 矿泉水 (kuàngquánshuǐ) is 'mineral water' — sold everywhere. 热水 (rè shuǐ) is 'hot water' — Chinese restaurants give it by default. 冰水 (bīng shuǐ) is 'ice water' — you usually have to ask. In China, people drink 开水 (kāishuǐ, boiled water) regularly.

content.name: hotel
content.side_e: Wǒmen de jiǔdiàn zài nǎr? Wǒ xiǎng huí jiǔdiàn.
content.side_f: Where is our hotel? I want to go back to the hotel.
content.side_g: 酒店 is the standard word for hotel. 宾馆 (bīnguǎn) is a slightly older/cheaper alternative. 前台 (qiántái) is 'front desk.' Useful phrase: 我住在XX酒店 (wǒ zhù zài XX jiǔdiàn, 'I'm staying at XX hotel') — helpful when giving taxi directions.

content.name: help
content.side_e: Nǐ kěyǐ bāng wǒ ge máng ma?
content.side_g: 帮 means 'help' and 忙 means 'busy/matter.' 帮忙 is the general way to ask for help. The polite full form is 你可以帮我个忙吗？(Can you do me a favor?). Shorter: 帮帮我 (bāngbang wǒ, 'help me'). People are generally very willing to help foreigners in China.

content.name: wife
content.side_e: Zhè shì wǒ tàitai. Wǒmen yìqǐ lái lǚyóu de.
content.side_f: This is my wife. We came traveling together.
content.side_g: 太太 is the polite/formal way to say 'wife' — great for introducing her to locals. 老婆 (lǎopo) is the casual/intimate version. 爱人 (àirén) is more formal/mainland usage. When people ask 这是你太太吗？you can say 是的 (shì de, 'yes').

content.name: get_lost
content.side_a: To get lost; to lose one's way
content.side_e: Wǒmen mílù le! Qǐngwèn, zhè shì nǎr?
content.side_f: We're lost! Excuse me, where is this?
content.side_g: 迷 means 'confused/lost' and 路 means 'road.' 迷路了 (mílù le) means 'got lost' — add 了 for the completed state. On hiking trails in Zhangjiajie, stick to marked paths. If lost: 我迷路了，请帮帮我 (Wǒ mílù le, qǐng bāngbang wǒ — 'I'm lost, please help me').

content.name: look_forward_to
content.side_a: To look forward to; to be excited for (something upcoming)
content.side_e: Wǒ hěn qīdài chī huǒguō! / 我很期待吃火锅！(I'm really excited for hot pot!)

dialogue_001.title: Arriving at the Hotel
dialogue_001.description: 小明 and 小美 check into their hotel after arriving in China

lines.a: 小明，我们的酒店到了！
lines.b: Xiǎo Míng, wǒmen de jiǔdiàn dào le!
lines.c: Xiaoming, we've arrived at our hotel!

lines.a: 太好了！太太，你累不累？
lines.b: Tài hǎo le! Tàitai, nǐ lèi bu lèi?
lines.c: Great! Wife, are you tired?

lines.a: 有一点累。我们是来旅游的，不是来跑步的！
lines.b: Yǒu yìdiǎn lèi. Wǒmen shì lái lǚyóu de, bú shì lái pǎobù de!
lines.c: A little tired. We're here to travel, not to run!

lines.a: 哈哈！我去前台，你先休息。
lines.b: Hāhā! Wǒ qù qiántái, nǐ xiān xiūxi.
lines.c: Haha! I'll go to the front desk, you rest first.

lines.a: 好的，你需要帮忙吗？
lines.b: Hǎo de, nǐ xūyào bāngmáng ma?
lines.c: OK, do you need help?

lines.a: 不用，我一个人可以。太太先坐一会儿吧。
lines.b: Bú yòng, wǒ yí ge rén kěyǐ. Tàitai xiān zuò yíhuìr ba.
lines.c: No need, I can handle it alone. Wife, sit down for a bit.

dialogue_002.title: Ordering Hot Pot
dialogue_002.description: 小明 and 小美 order dinner at a hot pot restaurant in Chongqing

lines.a: 这家火锅店看起来不错！
lines.b: Zhè jiā huǒguō diàn kàn qǐlai búcuò!
lines.c: This hot pot restaurant looks pretty good!

lines.a: 我们看看菜单吧。
lines.b: Wǒmen kànkan càidān ba.
lines.c: Let's look at the menu.

lines.a: 小明，这个太辣了，我吃不了。
lines.b: Xiǎo Míng, zhège tài là le, wǒ chī bu liǎo.
lines.c: Xiaoming, this is too spicy, I can't eat it.

lines.a: 没关系，我们点鸳鸯锅，一半辣一半不辣。
lines.b: Méi guānxi, wǒmen diǎn yuānyāng guō, yí bàn là yí bàn bú là.
lines.c: No worries, let's order a split pot, half spicy half not spicy.

lines.a: 好主意！再要两杯水。
lines.b: Hǎo zhǔyi! Zài yào liǎng bēi shuǐ.
lines.c: Good idea! Also get two glasses of water.

lines.a: 这个牛肉真好吃！
lines.b: Zhège niúròu zhēn hǎochī!
lines.c: This beef is really delicious!

dialogue_003.title: Hiking in Zhangjiajie
dialogue_003.description: 小明 and 小美 go hiking and enjoy the mountain scenery

lines.a: 今天我们去爬山吧！
lines.b: Jīntiān wǒmen qù pá shān ba!
lines.c: Let's go hiking today!

lines.a: 好啊！听说这里的风景很美。
lines.b: Hǎo a! Tīngshuō zhèlǐ de fēngjǐng hěn měi.
lines.c: Sure! I heard the scenery here is very beautiful.

lines.a: 小心！这里的路很滑。
lines.b: Xiǎoxīn! Zhèlǐ de lù hěn huá.
lines.c: Be careful! The path here is very slippery.

lines.a: 谢谢！你看，风景太美了，我想照相。
lines.b: Xièxie! Nǐ kàn, fēngjǐng tài měi le, wǒ xiǎng zhào xiàng.
lines.c: Thanks! Look, the scenery is so beautiful, I want to take a photo.

lines.a: 好，我帮你拍。你带水了吗？
lines.b: Hǎo, wǒ bāng nǐ pāi. Nǐ dài shuǐ le ma?
lines.c: OK, I'll take it for you. Did you bring water?

lines.a: 带了！爬山一定要带水。
lines.b: Dài le! Pá shān yídìng yào dài shuǐ.
lines.c: Yes! You must bring water when hiking.

dialogue_004.title: Finding the Restroom
dialogue_004.description: 小美 gets lost looking for the restroom and asks for help

lines.a: 小明，厕所在哪儿？
lines.b: Xiǎo Míng, cèsuǒ zài nǎr?
lines.c: Xiaoming, where is the restroom?

lines.a: 我不知道，你去问一下吧。
lines.b: Wǒ bù zhīdào, nǐ qù wèn yíxià ba.
lines.c: I don't know, go ask someone.

lines.a: 我去找了，可是我迷路了！
lines.b: Wǒ qù zhǎo le, kěshì wǒ mílù le!
lines.c: I went to look, but I got lost!

lines.a: 别着急，我帮你找。请问，厕所在哪儿？
lines.b: Bié zhāojí, wǒ bāng nǐ zhǎo. Qǐngwèn, cèsuǒ zài nǎr?
lines.c: Don't worry, I'll help you find it. Excuse me, where is the restroom?

lines.a: 谢谢你帮忙！下次我不会迷路了。
lines.b: Xièxie nǐ bāngmáng! Xià cì wǒ bú huì mílù le.
lines.c: Thank you for helping! Next time I won't get lost.

dialogue_005.title: Taking Photos at Scenic Spots
dialogue_005.description: 小明 and 小美 enjoy sightseeing and take photos together

lines.a: 太太，这里的风景真漂亮！
lines.b: Tàitai, zhèlǐ de fēngjǐng zhēn piàoliang!
lines.c: Wife, the scenery here is really beautiful!

lines.a: 是啊！我们一起照相吧。
lines.b: Shì a! Wǒmen yìqǐ zhào xiàng ba.
lines.c: Yes! Let's take a photo together.

lines.a: 请问，可以帮我们照相吗？
lines.b: Qǐngwèn, kěyǐ bāng wǒmen zhào xiàng ma?
lines.c: Excuse me, can you take a photo for us?

lines.a: 这次旅游太开心了！
lines.b: Zhè cì lǚyóu tài kāixīn le!
lines.c: This trip has been so much fun!

lines.a: 是啊，太太。下次我们再来旅游。
lines.b: Shì a, tàitai. Xià cì wǒmen zài lái lǚyóu.
lines.c: Yes, wife. Next time we'll travel here again.
